beats.text: Here's a plane slicing through the origin in three-dimensional space.
Pick any two vectors in that plane — call them u, pointing to one, one, zero, and v, pointing to zero, one, one.
Add them. u plus v lands at one, two, one. Watch the tip — it sits exactly in the plane. Closure under addition holds.
Now scale u by three. The arrow stretches to three, three, zero. Still in the plane. Closure under scalar multiplication holds.
Both rules check out — so this plane through the origin is a subspace of R-three.
